core_mechanism: Unbiased off-policy / importance-weighted (counterfactual) estimation of the difference in expected outcome between two sampling distributions, where the shared-support (overlap) region contributes cancelling terms so the estimator's variance scales with the divergence between the two distributions rather than the raw outcome variance
brief_reason: The abstract frames the randomised assignment as a meta-policy and uses Δ-off-policy estimation to obtain unbiased treatment-effect estimates whose variance scales with the divergence between policies (vanishing on the overlap where the two policies agree) rather than with raw outcome variance.
